danger_signals.visual_form: Soviet or Allied unit models on minimap and battlefield; distinct silhouettes by faction
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring defensive response or preemptive strike

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy production buildings visible on map; refinery and war factory structures
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent generating resources and reinforcements; escalating threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Depleted ore fields; sparse resource nodes on map
danger_signals.meaning: Economic pressure; reduced income threatens base sustainability

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual degradation of building models; smoke/fire effects on structures
danger_signals.meaning: Base under attack or previously damaged; vulnerability to further assault

danger_signals.visual_form: Gaps in perimeter where pillboxes or defenses should be placed
danger_signals.meaning: Exposure to infiltration or direct assault

safety_signals.visual_form: Defensive structures (pillboxes, towers) arrayed around player base
Active ore fields with visible gems/ore; active refineries processing materials
safety_signals.meaning: Stable economy; sufficient funds for unit production and repairs

safety_signals.visual_form: War factories and barracks visibly operational; unit production queues active
safety_signals.meaning: Capacity to generate military response to threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Uncontested oil derricks and map sectors without enemy presence
safety_signals.meaning: Expansion opportunity; additional passive income sources available

safety_signals.visual_form: Player's units clustered near base or organized in attack formations
safety_signals.meaning: Military readiness and defensive posture maintained

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hammer and sickle motif; Soviet star imagery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Soviet unit designs: Heavy Rhino Tank, V3 Rocket Launcher, Conscript infantry; bulkier, more angular aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Soviet-themed buildings with communist architecture; red color scheme on structures; Cyrillic text on interfaces

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Allied unit designs: G.I. infantry, lighter armor tanks, air/naval units; sleeker Western military aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Western-style military structures; blue color scheme; English text on interfaces; modern military architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse base with minimal structures; few unit production facilities visible; small ore reserves on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player operates with limited Mobile Construction Unit; must carefully deploy initial platoon; economic vulnerability forces defensive posture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Expanded base with multiple production buildings visible; increased unit density on battlefield; resource contention visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Soviet units mass-produced for rushes; Allied forces building specialized units; defensive structures proliferate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Advanced Allied units appearing (naval warfare units, late-game infantry); tech structures visible; terrain more contested
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Allied faction leverages late-game superiority; Soviet early-game advantage diminishes; strategic positioning becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units clustered near player perimeter; smoke and fire effects on structures; minimap shows concentrated enemy presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player shifts to defensive unit production; repair operations activated on damaged structures; emergency selling of buildings may occur

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ore fields heavily depleted or absent; refineries idle; production buildings non-functional due to low funds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to sell buildings for emergency income; unit production halts; base cannot sustain defenses

environmental_storytelling.example: Soviet bases feature brutalist, angular structures with hammer-and-sickle emblems; Allied bases display Western military sleekness with NATO-style markings
Heavy Soviet tanks (Rhino, V3 Launcher) visually communicate brute force; lighter Allied units suggest mobility and agility
Placement of ore fields and neutral oil derricks creates territorial chokepoints; resource scarcity signals contested zones

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Progressive visual degradation of structures; smoke clouds, fire effects indicate combat damage and vulnerability

environmental_storytelling.example: Red for Soviet, blue for Allied enables instant faction identification and tactical overview of battlefield control
Soviet Conscript vs. Allied G.I. have distinct visual profiles; tank designs immediately identifiable by side profile

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible traveling between ore field and refinery; indicates economic activity and income generation
Queued for unloading; suggests resources being processed; economic throughput visible
Large groups visible near barracks; indicates early-game rush strategy; economic priority on quantity over quality
Units garrisoned in structures or arrayed at perimeter; indicates defensive doctrine and infantry superiority
Slow movement but powerful impact; indicates commitment of heavy resources; late-mid game capability
Visual animation of resource processing; color activity indicators; shows economic throughput state
Build queue visible; unit emergence from structure; indicates military build-up and offensive capability
Static position indicates commitment to defensive posture in specific sector; visible fire arcs show coverage zones

notes: Red Alert 2 communicates world-state primarily through unit type visibility, faction color-coding, and resource scarcity. The game uses clear visual faction identity (Soviet red/brutalism vs. Allied blue/modernity) to enable instant threat assessment. Danger signals emerge from enemy unit concentration, base damage, and resource depletion. Safety manifests through intact defensive networks, active resource extraction, and maintained production capacity. Campaign structure (Boot Camp → Allied/Soviet) and nation-specific units add narrative layer to political conflict representation. Wikipedia source provides campaign overview and faction mechanics but limited detail on specific visual manifestation techniques.
